summary: Focused verification of 3 requested changes: (1) Recent Closures now shows exactly 6 cards in 2-row x 3-col grid (Product Manager, B2B Product Marketing Manager, Marketing Manager, Junior Fullstack Developer, Marketing Automation Specialist, GTM Consultant) - confirmed via DOM query (6 grid children) and code review of workvybData.js. 'Accountant' text does NOT appear in the #recent-closures section (verified via section-scoped text search); it only appears as 'Accountants' inside the unrelated Roles We Hire > Finance Hiring category tag list, which was never part of this change request. (2) Hero section top padding confirmed reduced (pt-6 sm:pt-8, was pt-14/pt-20) - screenshot shows tight spacing between sticky header and headline at 1920px. (3) All section components confirmed via grep to use py-14 sm:py-20 (was py-20 sm:py-28) site-wide. Regression pass: FAQ accordion opens/closes correctly, floating WhatsApp button visible and links to wa.me, footer renders with quick links, light theme intact. Responsive check at 1920px/768px/390px - no overlapping elements, hamburger menu works correctly on mobile.
critical_code_review_comments: RECENT_CLOSURES array in workvybData.js correctly has 6 entries (Accountant removed), matching requested content.
Hero.jsx padding reduced to 'pb-10 pt-6 sm:pb-14 sm:pt-8' as requested; confirmed visually tighter spacing under header.

All 11 section components (Process, FAQ, Solution, RolesWeHire, Comparison, Testimonials, RecentClosures, Problem, BottomForm, WhyWorkvyb, FinalCTA) verified via grep to use 'py-14 sm:py-20', consistent site-wide spacing reduction as requested.

Note: 'Accountants' still appears as a role tag under Roles We Hire > Finance Hiring category - this is expected/unrelated since only the Recent Closures card was requested to be removed, not the general role category listing.

success_rate.backend: N/A - no backend changes this round

context_for_next_testing_agent: This round's 3 targeted changes (6-card grid, reduced hero top padding, reduced site-wide section spacing) are all verified working correctly with no regressions found in FAQ, WhatsApp button, footer, or responsive layouts (1920/768/390px). No further testing needed unless new features/changes are introduced.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no bugs found in this round.
